characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Bunny-Ears Lawyer: Despite her questionable decisions and bumbling, Michael Scott-like personality, she is generally shown to be quite good at her job. Consistent Clothing Style: She comes to work dressed in dark-colored pantsuits adorned with various golden lapel pins. Married to the Job: "A Very Robust Personal Life" suggests this trope, and she fills her free time with various hobbies to avoid confronting her loneliness. Reasonable Authority Figure: Joyce is generally a pretty reasonable boss. Skewed Priorities: Joyce seems more focused on making the hospital into a "medical destination", to the point that she spent $300,000 on a new mammography machine that the hospital didn't need, rather than on making upgrades that the hospital desperately needs, such as a new computer system.

characters.name: Joyce
characters.description: Bunny-Ears Lawyer: Despite her questionable decisions and bumbling, Michael Scott-like personality, she is generally shown to be quite good at her job. Consistent Clothing Style: She comes to work dressed in dark-colored pantsuits adorned with various golden lapel pins. Married to the Job: "A Very Robust Personal Life" suggests this trope, and she fills her free time with various hobbies to avoid confronting her loneliness. Reasonable Authority Figure: Joyce is generally a pretty reasonable boss. Skewed Priorities: Joyce seems more focused on making the hospital into a "medical destination", to the point that she spent $300,000 on a new mammography machine that the hospital didn't need, rather than on making upgrades that the hospital desperately needs, such as a new computer system.

characters.name: Alex
characters.description: Control Freak: As supervising nurse, Alex struggles to delegate, often stepping into handle every task in sight rather than trusting the nurses she oversees Only Sane Woman: Alex tends to be in this role, to the point that Joyce heavily relies on her input for things such as remarks to a board meeting. Pet the Dog: After the Filipino nurses who'd been rebelling against Alex learn that she only makes three dollars more an hour than they do — thus making less, overall, because of her increased workload — and her husband's business is seasonalnotehe sells grass seed, they stop making her miserable and bring in canned goods as a peace offering.

characters.name: Bruce
characters.description: St. Denis' trauma surgeon, who is brimming with self confidence. Big Brother Mentor: With Matt's down home, rural naïveté, Matt sees Bruce in this light, while Bruce appreciates having a sidekick whom he can easily impress. Matt dubs them Buffalo Bruce and Matty the Kid, which Bruce finds dumb… until he "comes up with it" later in the episode. Big Man on Campus: When he and Ron attend a medical conference, he proves himself to be very popular, quickly making friends and hosting a well attended party. Of course, he then makes a mess of it because Ron, the only one he really wanted to impress, didn't want to come. Formerly Fat: "Listen to your Ladybugs" reveals that Bruce used to be fat as a kid. The teasing he got motivated him to lose weight and become theFitness Nuthe is now. Inferiority Superiority Complex: Despite his swaggering confidence, Bruce has averyfragile ego. Hence he likes to make sure people know how amazing he is.

characters.name: Serena
characters.description: A former travel nurse and Alex's best friend. She's beautiful, confident, and sassy, while also being childish and petty at times. Alpha Bitch: Is loved and respected by the staff and patients, having been called the Beyonce of St. Dennis in a Yelp review.Lovable Alpha Bitch: She doesn't quite qualify as aClass Princess, though, due to petty and manipulative aspects of her character. Lovable Alpha Bitch: She doesn't quite qualify as aClass Princess, though, due to petty and manipulative aspects of her character. Formerly Fat: A meta case, Kahyun Kim filmed the pilot before her dramatic weight loss, with it never being acknowledged in the show. Green-Eyed Monster: When she sees her previous travel nurse colleagues still doing fun and adventurous things as they move from hospital to hospital, she feels jealous that she's settled down. By the end of the episode she rememberswhyshe chose to settle here: she now has friends.

characters.name: Matt
characters.description: A brand-new floor nurse who is in way over his head at St. Denis. He comes from a very rural community and brings a naïve wholesomeness with him. Big Damn Kiss:When his wife Violet from back home comes to get him back, she requests he kiss her to prove that they really have lost their spark. The passionate kiss that follows surprises Ron and Serena. Later, he confides in Serena that he held back so Valerie would feel it's overHappens again betweenMatt and Serenain "Here a Righteous Woman Comes." Happens again betweenMatt and Serenain "Here a Righteous Woman Comes." Clark Kent Outfit: When he has to remove his scrubs to get a wound treated, he is shown to be quite fit. Appropriate, considering his rural farmer background. He even references raising barns back home. The Fundamentalist: He comes from a fundamentalist Christian community that frowns upon modern medicine — he even lost a cousin to strep throat, an easily treatable condition in the21stcentury, because the cousin refused to seek treatment. Although he's taken a radical step by becoming a nurse and working in medicine, he still has some lingering beliefs that clash with those of his colleagues, such that organ donation is wrong because he fears arriving in heaven full of holes and empty spaces.Serena remarks that he may have been raised in such a fundamentalist community, that it may actually be a cult. Based on the anecdotes he casually brings up, she may be right.

characters.name: Val
characters.description: A nurse administrator, who usually gets the brunt of patient anger. Awful Wedded Life: In "Smile and Nod", Alex finds out that Val lied about being on jury duty in order to take a vacation to Cabo with her wife. When pressed for an explanation, she tells Alex that her relationship is under significant strain from all the hours she works, she suspects that her wife is cheating on her, and whisking her wife off to Cabo for a few days of sun and sex was the only thing she could think of to stop herself from blowing up at patients. Alex decides to drop the matter. Butt-Monkey: Her role forces her to juggle wave after wave of angry patients tired of the hospital's long wait times with no assistance from anyone else on staff. Twofer Token Minority: She is an Hawaiian-American lesbian.

characters.name: Ron
characters.description: An emergency physician who's seen it all. Jade-Colored Glasses: Ron's been a doctor for too long to be optimistic about much and Joyce's relentless cheeriness and bold ideas don't do much to brighten his mood. However, he still tirelessly plugs along, treating patients to the utmost. Married to the Job: Ron admits that his dedication to emergency medicine has wreaked havoc on his personal life and that his job is pretty much all he has going for him. He sees the same thing slowly happening to Alex and cautions her to make time for her family.
